description_long: This sculpture depicts a rider mounted on a horse, both rendered in a stylized, somewhat abstract form. The rider sits upright, with one arm extended outward, while the horse appears to be in mid-stride, giving a sense of motion and dynamism. The entire figure is mounted on a rectangular pedestal with a stepped base, suggesting a formal public installation. The surface of the sculpture is smooth with subtle textural variations, and the coloration is predominantly a cool gray with some darker accents. The base appears to be made of stone or concrete, providing a solid foundation for the metallic-looking rider and horse. The overall impression is one of monumentality and commemoration, typical of equestrian statues found in urban plazas or parks.
description: A stylized equestrian statue featuring a rider with an outstretched arm atop a striding horse, mounted on a rectangular stone pedestal. The sculpture has a smooth, metallic finish with a solid, stepped base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rider's outstretched arm and the horse's head facing forward, with the pedestal's inscription area visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full profile of the horse and rider, emphasizing the dynamic pose and the length of the horse's stride.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the rider and horse, with the pedestal's rear face and base structure clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horse's other profile and the rider's posture, mirroring the dynamic movement seen in view 1.
attribute_subject_matter: equestrian statue of a rider on horseback
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is mounted on a formal pedestal, suggesting an outdoor urban or park setting, possibly in a plaza or public square.
